evals.prompt: validate the analogies used in the pro side's debate turn
evals.expected_behavior: Check structural requirements, content share, classify pass/fail

evals.prompt: check if the 2008 financial crisis analogy has enough similarities and differences
evals.expected_behavior: Verify >=2 similarities, >=1 key difference, assess relevance

evals.prompt: audit historical references in round 2 for classical reference compliance
evals.expected_behavior: Apply strict or advisory mode rules based on section context

evals.prompt: assess the analogy content share percentage in this debate turn
evals.expected_behavior: Estimate ~15% threshold via LLM semantic judgment

evals.prompt: validate historical parallel structure in the con argument
evals.expected_behavior: Check structural completeness, flag heuristic-only analogies

evals.prompt: verify the historical wisdom section meets advisory mode requirements
evals.expected_behavior: Check key_differences and applicability_caveat fields, grade quality

evals.prompt: write an analogy to explain recursion to a beginner
evals.expected_behavior: This is pedagogy, not debate analogy validation

evals.prompt: give me a historical example of successful tech migrations
evals.expected_behavior: This is a research request, not analogy compliance checking

evals.prompt: compare these two code patterns and tell me which is better
evals.expected_behavior: This is code comparison, not historical analogy validation

evals.prompt: use a metaphor to explain this complex concept
evals.expected_behavior: This is explanation request, not debate analogy safeguard
